results.name: has_hook
results.detail: "Good olive oil has a *natural anti-inflammatory* i"

rationales.hook_strength: The ibuprofen comparison is a concrete, surprising hook that earns the stop — loses a point because olive oil content is crowded and the peppery-sting angle has been circulating for a few years.
rationales.clarity: Each slide is a single actionable idea with a clear label, no jargon overload, and the buy/use tips are specific enough to act on this week.
rationales.audience_fit: Busy 20–35s who already use olive oil will find this immediately applicable, though the oleocanthal naming risks losing the most casual viewer on slide 2.
rationales.novelty: The oleocanthal-ibuprofen parallel is well-trodden in nutrition content; the peppery-sting-as-quality-signal framing is the freshest angle but not new enough to score higher.
rationales.brand_fit: Claims are properly hedged ('gently, over time', 'adds up'), no hype language, CTA ties the specific upgrade to the Health Score naturally without feeling forced.
rationales.predicted_performance: High save potential — actionable buying and usage tips drive bookmarks — but shares may be moderate since the ibuprofen comparison is familiar enough that many viewers will feel they already knew this.
